name: Spain
contextBlurb: Spain's housing portfolio is led by Isabel Rodríguez (PSOE), Ministra de Vivienda y Agenda Urbana. The Spanish housing market remains a central public-debate issue, with initiatives oriented toward affordable housing and rental regulation. The BORME (Spain's official commercial gazette) reflects company-formation activity related to housing and rental, although the available snippets correspond mainly to commercial registrations rather than parliamentary bills.

legislation.title: Constitución de MADRID AFFORDABLE HOUSING 2021 SA
legislation.summary: Commercial registration of Madrid Affordable Housing 2021 SA, an entity tied to the promotion of affordable housing in Madrid. Its corporate activity reflects private initiatives operating within affordable-housing policy.

legislation.title: Acto registral de KEYGO RENT SPAIN SA
legislation.summary: Registration of Keygo Rent Spain SA, a company operating in the Spanish rental market. Reflects ongoing rental-sector activity within the broader Spanish housing market context.

legislation.title: Acto registral de KH LA VIVIENDA
legislation.summary: BORME registration reference for KH La Vivienda, an entity tied to the housing sector in Spain. Reflects the continued activity of companies operating in the housing space.

keyFigures.name: Isabel Rodríguez
keyFigures.role: Ministra de Vivienda y Agenda Urbana
